communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Berkeley public records request #26-647
communications.communication:
City of Berkeley ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-647: This is in response to your March 3, 2026 Public Records Act request No. 26-647. City staff have identified records responsive to your request. Staff anticipate being able to provide all responsive records no later than Tuesday, April 7th at 5 p.m.   The Mayor’s Form 700s are filed with and maintained by the state. (Gov. Code § 87200.) They are publicly available through the California Fair Political Practices Commission and can be viewed online via the following link: https://www.fppc.ca.gov/search-filings/form-700-search/search-filed-form-700s/ If you have any questions concerning your request, please feel free to contact me at kperez@berkeleyca.gov. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Berkeley.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Berkeley public records request #26-647
communications.communication:
City of Berkeley ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-647: This is in response to your March 3, 2026 Public Records Act request No. 26-647. Your request is granted. The City has completed its search for responsive documents and as a courtesy has attached them to this letter. Additionally, portions of these documents have been redacted to protect personal information. (Cal. Gov. Code §§ 7922.000 (formerly Cal. Gov. Code §§ 6255.)) Certain documents and portions of documents have been withheld based upon the deliberative process and attorney-client privileges, and the privilege for preliminary notes and drafts. (Cal. Gov. Code §§ 7922.000 & 7927.705 & 7927.500). Please note that the Public Records Act defines “public records” to include records pertaining to City business which are “prepared, owned, used, or retained” by the City. (Cal. Gov. Code § 7920.530.) The Act therefore does not require the City to search for, collect or disclose personal records predating an official assuming office with the City. The Mayor’s Form 700s are filed with and maintained by the state. (Gov. Code § 87200.) They are publicly available through the California Fair Political Practices Commission and can be viewed online via the following link: https://www.fppc.ca.gov/search-filings/form-700-search/search-filed-form-700s/ With this response, staff have completed their work to process and respond to your Public Records Act request. If you have any questions concerning your request, please feel free to contact me at kperez@berkeleyca.gov.   ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Berkeley.

Technical support: See our help page

title: The Burns', Jeffrey Epstein & Ghislaine Maxwell (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: The Burns', Jeffrey Epstein & Ghislaine Maxwell (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1.) Any and all records relating to the meetings between ex-CIA director William Burns. According to https://www.rollingstone.com/culture/culture-news/cia-director-noam-chomsky-epstein-calendar-1234726393/ The calendar showed that in 2014, while current CIA director William Burns was deputy secretary of state, Burns had three meetings scheduled with Epstein, first meeting in Washington and then at Epstein’s Manhattan townhouse. Rumors and accusations have long swirled around Epstein that he was some kind of intelligence asset.

2.) Any and all records relating to Thomas E. Burns Jr., according to https://www.buzzfeednews.com/article/rosiegray/jeffrey-epstein-state-department “As you are aware, Mr. Epstein’s apparent departure from the house and his failure to make timely rental payments in February and March of this year have been matters of serious concern to this office,” wrote Thomas E. Burns Jr., then the deputy director of the Office of Foreign Missions, in April 1996 in a letter attached to court filings. Burns wrote that the OFM had already found someone to take over the lease from Epstein, a developer named Xenophon Galinas, and that they would not approve the sublease to Fisher.

3.) Any and all records relating to Ghislaine Maxwell and her alias "Dana Burns". According to https://www.thesun.co.uk/news/10469504/prince-andrew-ghislaine-maxwell-virginia/ "Then there is Ellmax, a company she set up in 2012. Its registered UK address is a cottage in Wiltshire occupied by Catherine Vaughan Edwards, another old friend. Its U.S. base is Maxwell’s New York flat, via one ‘Dana Burns’, which appears to be an alias that Ghislaine uses." https://web.archive.org/web/20250729205211/https://static.foxnews.com/foxnews.com/content/uploads/2024/01/10.pdf

4.) Any and all records regarding FBI Special Agent Brian A. Burns, Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell. Upon information and belief, Agent Burns was an FBI Agent in Tennessee before being relocated to Buffalo, NY.
https://www.actionnews5.com/story/6358294/fbi-agent-says-bureau-didnt-know-about-tim-willis-side-project-during-waltz/
https://artvoice.com/2016/09/judge-pietruszka-role-assigning-counsel-witness-fbi-tried-flip-pigeon

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

The Riford Family

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: RAW RIPA Data (Beverly Hills Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

I am requesting your department's state mandated RIPA stop data from 2020 to today's date. Please upload in .csv or Excel format. No PDFs. 

Thank you

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: Audit letters of compliance for Danbury, CT 2018-2024

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Audit letters of compliance for Danbury, CT 2018-2024
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Management Letters: All final "Management Letters" or "Letters to Those Charged with Governance" issued by the City’s external auditors (including but not limited to CliftonLarsonAllen LLP) for the fiscal years ending 2018 through 2025.

2. Internal Control Deficiencies: Any written communications from external auditors regarding "significant deficiencies" or "material weaknesses" in the City’s internal controls related to departmental compliance with the City Charter, specifically regarding the qualification of officers under Charter Section 2-5.

3. Compliance Testing Records: Any audit "working papers" or summaries in the City's possession that document the testing of Oaths of Office for the Health Department Directors (Fernanda Carvalho, Kara Prunty, and Lisa Morrissey).

4. Single Audit Compliance: For the years 2018–2024, please provide the "Report on Compliance for Each Major State/Federal Program" that specifically addresses the Health Department’s adherence to administrative legal requirements.

Public Interest & Urgency: These records are of vital public interest following the $17 million verdict in Girolametti v. Danbury, which established a pattern of "reckless disregard" within City departments. The failure of three consecutive Health Directors to file mandatory Oaths of Office suggests a systemic failure of internal controls that the auditors were tasked to identify.

I request a response within the statutory four business days. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption and provide the name of the official responsible for the denial.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Paige

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Audit letters of compliance for Danbury, CT 2018-2024
communications.communication:
This request is now in a state of "Deemed Denial" under CGS § 1-206(a), as more than four business days have passed without a production of records or a valid legal explanation for their withholding.

Please be advised that per the Connecticut Freedom of Information Commission's strict 30-day jurisdictional window, I will be filing a formal Notice of Appeal if these records are not received by February 27, 2026. I would prefer to resolve this via this portal, but I must act to preserve my state-mandated appeal rights.

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Marquette County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Marquette County)
communications.communication:
Dear Marquette County FOIA Assistant FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Marquette County FOIA Assistant county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would
inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I
would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if
available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro

734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

title: Open Records Request for ALPR Documents -  Bloomington Police Indiana

communications.subject: Indiana Access to Public Records Act Request: Open Records Request for ALPR Documents -  Bloomington Police Indiana
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to Indiana’s Access to Public Records Act (IC 5-14-3), I respectfully request a copy of the following records held by your agency related to the use of automated license plate readers (ALPRs) for the period of January 1, 2022, to December 9, 2025. To ensure compliance with IC 5-14-3-3, I request that these records be provided in PDF format, where available, to facilitate public access and minimize costs. If PDF format is unavailable, paper records are acceptable. If any portion of this request is denied or redacted, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption under IC 5-14-3-4 and any applicable case law, as required by IC 5-14-3-9.

Please provide copies of the following records:

1. Contracts with ALPR Vendors: All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, or other documents between your agency and any company providing ALPR technology or services, including but not limited to Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions, Rekor Systems, or any other ALPR vendor. This includes any amendments, renewals, or extensions of such contracts executed or effective between January 1, 2022, and December 9, 2025.

2. Policies on ALPR Data Access: All policies, procedure manuals, training materials, or other documentation, effective or revised between January 1, 2022, and December 9, 2025, that describe: a. Who within your agency (e.g., specific roles, ranks, or units) is authorized to search or access license plate data collected by any ALPR vendor, regardless of whether the data is stored by the vendor or the Monroe County Sheriff’s Office. b. The process for granting, revoking, or monitoring such access.

3. Policies on ALPR Searches and Alerts: All policies, procedure manuals, or other documentation, effective or revised between January 1, 2022, and December 9, 2025, that describe: a. The circumstances under which a license plate may be searched in ALPR data, including any criteria, approvals, or justifications required. b. The circumstances under which an alert or notification may be set for a specific license plate, including any criteria, approvals, or justifications required.

4. Policies on ALPR Search Logs and Audits: All policies, procedure manuals, or other documentation, effective or revised between January 1, 2022, and December 9, 2025, that describe: a. The maintenance of logs recording who searches ALPR data, when, and the stated reason or purpose for each search. b. The process, frequency, and criteria for auditing such logs to ensure compliance with your agency's policies or state/federal law, including any reports or documentation summarizing audit procedures.

If any records are maintained in a database or electronic system, I request an export of the relevant data in a machine-readable format (e.g., CSV, Excel) pursuant to IC 5-14-3-3(g), converted to PDF for delivery. If any responsive records are withheld or redacted, please provide a detailed log describing each withheld or redacted record, including the record’s title, date, and the specific legal basis for withholding or redaction.

I am willing to pay reasonable fees for copying or searching records, as permitted under IC 5-14-3-8, up to a maximum of $100. Please notify me in advance if fees are expected to exceed this amount. If a fee waiver is available due to the public interest in understanding your agency’s use of ALPR technology for transparency and accountability, I respectfully request consideration under IC 5-14-3-8(e). The requested information is sought for non-commercial purposes.

Please provide the requested records within the statutory timeline outlined in IC 5-14-3-9 (seven business days for acknowledgment). If you have any questions or need clarification, please contact me. Thank you for your attention to this request, and I look forward to your prompt response.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Ratcliff

Have a wonderful day.

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Grays Harbor County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Grays Harbor County, WA. - Records Request 2026-139 Fee Letter
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
  
 In an effort to provide you the most timely records and fullest assistance, the County has completed its review of one electronic file (Brady List) responsive to your request. As this electronic file is under the charging threshold, the electronic file is being released today with no fees. You can access your records via the link here: Request Number: 2026-139 
  
 Pursuant to RCW 42.56.080, this email will serve as formal notice that the County will be producing installments of records for your request. Please note, that the County will not prepare the next installment of records until the current Production has been paid for. The County anticipates a minimum of two Productions for your request. 
  
 The County has now completed its search for Production #1 and the following records have been located: 
  
 Paper (needs scanning): 154 pages 
  
 The fee to receive these records electronically is: $15.40 
  
 154 x $0.10 = $15.40 
  
 *If you wish to be provided hard copy records, please reply for updated fees. 
  
 Records will not be available the same day that payment is made. The County does its best to deliver records within (10) business days of receipt of payment. The additional time is needed to review the records for potential redactions prior to release. 
  
 The County currently accepts cash, check, or credit/debit card. Credit/debit card payments are only available as an online option. Please note that a convenience charge is added for this method of payment. We are not able to accept in person credit/debit card payments. 
  
 The information that you will need (in addition to information pertaining to the credit/debit card holder) is as follows: 
  
 PRR Number: 2026-139

 Electronic (existing): 97 files

title: JPD's Flock Audit Reports

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: JPD's Flock Audit Reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All audit reports created as a result of auditing Joplin Police Department's Flock system.  This should include the audit report created as a result of the audit mentioned in the Flock misuse press release dated 1/10/2026.  This should also include all audit reports conducted on a monthly basis since then as indicated by the press release.  Audit reports are not audit logs.  Audit reports are a report of the audit that was performed using audit logs.  These reports would include things like anomalies found, anomalies sustained, anomalies cleared, status of access controls, department pass/fail result, auditor name, and so on.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

title: NYDOT: Certified Passenger Carrier List

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NYDOT: Certified Passenger Carrier List
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request the following records maintained by the NYSDOT Office of Modal Safety & Security, Passenger Carrier Safety Bureau:

A list of all passenger carriers currently certified or previously certified by NYSDOT, including the following fields for each carrier:

Carrier name (legal name and DBA if different)
NYSDOT Operator ID / Certification number
Certification type (school bus, motorcoach, ambulette, for-hire passenger, charter, etc.)
Certification status (active, suspended, revoked, expired, pending)
Certification effective date
Certification expiration date (if applicable)
Suspension or revocation date and reason (if applicable)
Business address (street, city, state, zip)
County
NYSDOT region
USDOT number (if on file)
Number of vehicles authorized

I request this data in electronic format, preferably as a CSV or pipe-delimited text file. If the full dataset is too large for email delivery, I am willing to receive it via a file-sharing link or on physical media.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide the specific statutory basis for the denial and release the remaining non-exempt portions.

I am willing to discuss narrowing the scope of this request if it is deemed unreasonably burdensome. Please contact me at [email/phone] to discuss.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ian White

communications.communication:

communications.subject: Acknowledgement :: R010044-021626
communications.communication:
RE: FOIL Records Request of February 16, 2026, Reference # R010044-021626 
Dear Ian White, 
This correspondence will acknowledge receipt of your February 16, 2026 Freedom of Information (FOIL) request at the New York State Department of Transportation (NYSDOT) Records Access Office on this date. 
You requested: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records: 

I request the following records maintained by the NYSDOT Office of Modal Safety & Security, Passenger Carrier Safety Bureau: 

A list of all passenger carriers currently certified or previously certified by NYSDOT, including the following fields for each carrier: 

Carrier name (legal name and DBA if different) 
NYSDOT Operator ID / Certification number 
Certification type (school bus, motorcoach, ambulette, for-hire passenger, charter, etc.) 
Certification status (active, suspended, revoked, expired, pending) 
Certification effective date 
Certification expiration date (if applicable) 
Suspension or revocation date and reason (if applicable) 
Business address (street, city, state, zip) 
County 
NYSDOT region 
USDOT number (if on file) 
Number of vehicles authorized 

I request this data in electronic format, preferably as a CSV or pipe-delimited text file. If the full dataset is too large for email delivery, I am willing to receive it via a file-sharing link or on physical media. 

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide the specific statutory basis for the denial and release the remaining non-exempt portions. 

I am willing to discuss narrowing the scope of this request if it is deemed unreasonably burdensome. Please contact me at [email/phone] to discuss. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Ian White 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2219151/" 
We are researching your request and will notify you within the next 20 business days regarding the availability of the records you are seeking. 
Sincerely, 
New York State Department of Transportation

The NYS Department of Transportation has reviewed its files and has located records responsive to Items 1 and 2 of your request. Please log in to the FOIL Records Access Center at the following link to retrieve the responsive records. FOIL Records Request - R010044-021626 
Certain records you are seeking, or portions thereof, have been redacted pursuant to § 87(2)(b) of the Public Officers Law as disclosure of those portions would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy as described in § 89(2)(b). 
The records you requested for Item 3 of the request are being withheld pursuant to § 87(2)(g) of the Public Officers Law as they consist of inter-agency or intra-agency materials which are not i. statistical or factual tabulations or data; ii. instructions to staff that affect the public; iii. final agency policy or determinations; or iv. external audits.  They are pre-decisional in nature, are integral to the Department’s deliberative process, and contain opinions, evaluations, deliberations, policy formulations, proposals, conclusions, recommendations or other subjective matter.  Accordingly, access to these documents, and/or portions thereof, is hereby denied. 
A diligent search of our files failed to reveal any records that are responsive to Item 4 of your request. 
Under provisions of the Public Officers Law you may appeal this determination.  If you desire to submit such an appeal, please forward a copy of the original request and a copy of this response with your written appeal to:

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Oyster River Cooperative School District)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Oyster River Cooperative School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

The Right-To-Know Law provides that most e-mail communication, to or from School District employees regarding the business of the School District, are governmental records available to the public upon request.  Therefore, this e-mail communication may be subject to public disclosure.  However, confidential information about students, staff, and parents may not be subject to disclosure.  The confidentiality of information about students and personnel matters must be maintained by the individual or entity to which this e-mail is addressed.  Any unauthorized review, use, disclosure, or distribution of confidential information is prohibited.

title: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Borough Manager's Office)

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Borough Manager's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident location, area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (George Mason University)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (George Mason University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (University Of Hawaii, West Oahu)

communications.subject: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Library Contract Request: HeinOnline (University Of Hawaii, West Oahu)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with William S. Hein & Co. relating to the licensing of the “HeinOnline” database;
2) All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; and
3) All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with William S. Hein & Co. for the “HeinOnline” database. 

Please do not limit your search to strictly direct agreements or renewals between the institution and William S. Hein & Co., but include any agreements and related documentation through consortia such as the Hawai’i Library Consortium, the Greater Western Library Alliance (GWLA), or Lyrasis.

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of the “HeinOnline” database.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ellen Dubinsky

title: WA ALPR: A: External Access Audit Logs (1/4) (Port of Seattle Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: WA ALPR: A: External Access Audit Logs (1/4) (Port of Seattle Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All system audit logs, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting access to your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data (e.g., Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, Motorola/Vigilant), by users affiliated with any agency other than your agency.

2. All system audit logs, as natively maintained, reflecting the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) or agency name associated with each external query of your agency's ALPR system.

3. All system audit logs, as natively maintained, reflecting the user identifier, badge number, or account name associated with each query of your agency's ALPR system, whether the querying user was internal or external to your agency.

4. All records identifying which external agencies are currently authorized to access, query, or receive data from your agency's ALPR system, including any configuration record, permissions list, access control list, or authorized-agency registry maintained in your ALPR platform.  If this information exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot, faithful reproduction, or export of that display to any available file format is an acceptable and required form of production.

Note on vendor-held records: In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure. Vendor custody does not relieve your agency of its production obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

Note on record creation: I am seeking existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Agencies cannot evade their production obligation by characterizing screenshots of existing system displays as "new records."

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

title: James Pinjuv Email Metadata

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: James Pinjuv Email Metadata
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Metadata for all email communications which included "jimmerpinjuv@gmail.com" as either the sender and/or receiver, including being CCed or BCCed, between 01/01/2025 and the date this request is processed.

The specific metadata data points I'm interested in are: to address(es), from address, cc address(es), bcc address(es), sent date/time, received date/time, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, and only the specified email metadata. Please do not include duplicate emails. Please provide me with the records in an Excel format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: CLERK CORRESPONDENCE TEMPLATES / STANDARD LANGUAGE
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. §§ 19.31–19.39.

I request copies of any form letters, templates, standardized correspondence, or model language used or recommended for clerk communications regarding:

• “pay the filing fee or show good cause” notices,
• conditional fee-waiver communications, or
• correspondence addressing filing-fee obligations following appellate procedural changes (including appeal-to-writ conversion).

This request seeks administrative records only and does not request records concerning judicial reasoning or case-specific adjudication.

If responsive records exist in multiple versions or revisions, please provide the most current version and any prior versions still in use during the past five years.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

title: Suspension Files, Updated

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Suspension Files, Updated
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Suspensions issued by schools in the 2025-2026 school year.

Provide the name of the school and break down the data by grade within each school. Include the student code violation.  

Please note the Aspen codes used:

In-School Suspension (ISS)
Suspension (A-E SUS)

All of this information is contained in the student information system, Aspen. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19304

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019304-032226
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
04/06/2026 
Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019304-032226 
Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. Suspensions issued by schools in the 2025-2026 school year. 

Provide the name of the school and break down the data by grade within each school. Include the student code violation. 

Please note the Aspen codes used: 

In-School Suspension (ISS) 
Suspension (A-E SUS) 

All of this information is contained in the student information system, Aspen. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Attachments: 

https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/miriamcb/173676/Wehmhoefer.DunbarHS.9537_REDACTED_RELEASE.pdf 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2246844/ 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please be advised that the District does not comprehensively track this information by grade. Thus, this portion of your request is asking the District to create a new record to answer your question. 
The Freedom of Information Act requires a public body to provide existing public records, subject to certain exceptions. The request must reasonably describe the requested records. A reasonable description requires the record to be reasonably identified as a record not as a general request for data, information and statistics to be gleaned generally from documents which have not been created and which the [public body] does not generally create. (See Krohn v. Department of Justice, 628 F.2d 195 (1980)). Second,  FOIA obligations apply to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request. Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32,    540 N.E.2d 11, 13 (1989) (quoting Krohn v. Department of Justice, 628 F.2d 195, 198 (D.C. Cir. 1980)). This request requires CPS to not merely pull data out of a database and provide it to you, but instead asks CPS to compile data from multiple sources into a single record, manipulate it to answer your questions, and this combination of data does not already exist in any CPS database. (See generally Hites v. Waubonsee Comm. College, Il App 2d, 150836 (2016)(... "requests went beyond a search for records—that is, the data in the databases—and instead improperly sought information about those records." quoting National Security Counselors v. Central Intelligence Agency, 898 F. Supp. 2d 233, 271 (D.D.C. 2012) and Chicago Tribune v. DFPR, IL App. (4th) 130437 at 8 (2014) ("[t]he Department was not obligated ... to answer plaintiff's "general inquiry question" concerning the number of initial claims since this would have required creating a new record.") 
You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: York High School, CPS Principal Personnel File

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: York High School, CPS Principal Personnel File
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Please supply the full personnel files of the following individuals:

Sharnette Sims, Principal at York High School

This includes any resumes, applications, any job recommendations, promotions, professional license credentials and verification records. Please include a work/salary history. Please include any investigative materials - investigative memorandums and interview summary reports, disciplinary actions (suspensions, leaves of absence that stem from disciplinary action), incident reports, EOCO investigations, Office of Civil Rights determinations, non-disciplinary memorandum of understandings, non-union misconduct form, full warning resolutions and any complaints or claims/lawsuits/settlements. Please include any safety plans. Please include any separation agreements and any other disclosure agreements.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

--- Please respond above this line --- 
04/06/2026 
Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019307-032226 
Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

Please supply the full personnel files of the following individuals: 

Sharnette Sims, Principal at York High School 

This includes any resumes, applications, any job recommendations, promotions, professional license credentials and verification records. Please include a work/salary history. Please include any investigative materials - investigative memorandums and interview summary reports, disciplinary actions (suspensions, leaves of absence that stem from disciplinary action), incident reports, EOCO investigations, Office of Civil Rights determinations, non-disciplinary memorandum of understandings, non-union misconduct form, full warning resolutions and any complaints or claims/lawsuits/settlements. Please include any safety plans. Please include any separation agreements and any other disclosure agreements. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2247077/ 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please be advised that certain private information (personal descriptions, employee ID numbers, college transcripts, social security numbers, signatures, personal addresses, health information, etc.) was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a) exempts “ information specifically prohibited from disclosure by federal or State Law … ” (5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a)) Specifically, 105 ILCS 5/10-21.9 (b) states that “ any information concerning the record of convictions obtained by the president of the school board or the regional superintendent shall be confidential and may only be transmitted to the superintendent of the school district or his designee, the appropriate regional superintendent if the check was requested by the school district, the presidents of the appropriate school boards if the check was requested from the Department of State Police by the regional superintendent, the State Superintendent of Education, the State Teacher Certification Board, any other person necessary to the decision of hiring the applicant for employment, or for clarification purposes the Department of State Police or Statewide Sex Offender Database, or both.” 
Portions of this request that include personnel evaluations are redacted entirely in accordance with section 7(1)(a) of FOIA (5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a)) which exempts from public release information specifically prohibited from disclosure by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or State law and also in accordance with section 7.5(q) of the FOIA (5 ILCS 140/7.5(q)) which exempts from public release information prohibited from being disclosed by the Personnel Records Review Act [820 ILCS 40/0.01 et seq.] Section 7.5(h) exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). 
Redactions have been made pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r), which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA prohibits the disclosure of students and any student records in which a student may be individually identified by a school regardless of how or where the information pursuant to (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Redactions have also been made in accordance with 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(j)(iii) which exempts from release information concerning a school or university’s adjudication of student disciplinary cases, but only to the extent that disclosure would unavoidably reveal the identity of the student. Names and personal information of complainants and victims have been redacted pursuant to section 7(d)(iv) of the FOIA which exempts confidential information furnished only by the confidential source, or persons who file complaints with or provide information to administrative, investigative, law enforcement, or penal agencies. You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)
